ACS child father-presence proxy by race, poverty, and income.
ACS, CPS, and SIPP weighted context table.
Narrative synthesis for documentation and frontend copy.

key_findings.text: Father-absent respondents score -0.37 SD lower on g_proxy; -0.19 SD persists after covariate adjustment. Education gap: -1.3 years raw, -1.2 years adjusted.
Overall $19,053 gap. Largest race-adjusted earnings gap is -$17,126 for Non-Black non-Hispanic.
ACS 2024: 59.2% of children below poverty lack a resident father vs 7.6% in the top income quartile.

prevalence.insight: The adjusted earnings gap is largest in Non-Black non-Hispanic (-$17,126). The cognitive gap remains negative across race groups after adjustment.

outcomes.cognition_insight: Father-absent respondents score -0.37 SD lower on cognitive composite and complete -1.3 fewer years of education. After adjustment, the g gap is -0.19 SD and the education gap is -1.16 years.

faq.prevalence_answer: 43.2% of respondents in the NLSY97 cohort (born 1980–84) experienced father absence by this definition. In ACS 2024 data on current children under 18, 25.6% lack a resident father.
faq.earnings_answer: Father-present adults earn $70,784 on average versus $51,731 for father-absent adults — a $19,053 gap.
faq.national_answer: NLSY97 mean earnings ($63,003) are higher than ACS ($55,894) and CPS ($53,341) estimates for similar ages.

memos.cross_cohort_synthesis:
# Cross-Cohort Synthesis

This memo is built only from committed appendix and benchmark artifacts.

## Inputs
- Appendix manifest artifacts: 18
- Normalized summary rows: 278
- Forest-ready rows: 149

## Headline Rows
- acs_child_context_table / acs_child_context_table / father_absent_share: 0.2556
- benchmark_context_table / acs_pums / weighted_employment_share: 0.8115
- benchmark_context_table / acs_pums / weighted_poverty_share: 0.1058
- benchmark_context_table / cps_asec / weighted_employment_share: 0.8062
- benchmark_context_table / cps_asec / weighted_employment_share: 0.8037
- benchmark_context_table / cps_asec / weighted_employment_share: 0.8054
- benchmark_context_table / cps_asec / weighted_poverty_share: 0.1001
- benchmark_context_table / cps_asec / weighted_poverty_share: 0.1008

## Notes
- The summary table keeps amount and share rows together so downstream consumers can filter by `value_kind`.
- The forest-ready table only includes rows with an interval estimate, which keeps it usable for plotting without raw-data recalculation.
- Predictor terms remain on the log-odds scale in the forest-ready layer.
